high_risk_growth.reason: Only candidate that can fill MDD budgets >15%. Simplest (3 params). Well-proven baseline.

low_risk_capital_efficient.reason: Highest Sharpe (1.31) with moderate complexity (8 params). More capacity than LATCH (+34%).
low_risk_capital_efficient.evidence_grade: TENTATIVE (advantage over scaled E0 not proven at 95%)

research_control.reason: 8 params vs LATCH 15. Equivalent performance. Easier to audit and validate.

high_risk_growth.reason: Can partially fill medium-risk budgets with higher Sharpe than E0

low_risk_capital_efficient.reason: Safety fallback; lower tentative CAGR at matched MDD but proven capacity

research_control.reason: Simplest (3 params) but requires exposure scaling for low-risk use

deprecated_candidates.reason: Zero complexity premium over SM (Sharpe +0.003, P(>0)=55%). 99.8% same signal, double the parameters. Replaced by SM.
Dominated by SM on Sharpe (1.24 vs 1.31), Calmar (0.85 vs 0.93), and risk capacity (12.7% vs 15.0% MDD ceiling).

claims_robust: Scoring formula has structural exposure bias (CAGR term = 108-182% of delta)
35% of original score gap is sizing/exposure not signal quality
SM and LATCH are 99.8% concordant at default parameters
LATCH complexity premium over SM is absent (Sharpe +0.003)
E0 has highest native risk capacity (MDD ceiling 63.3%)
LATCH cannot reach E0-level risk without leverage
P is dominated by SM on all major metrics
claims_tentative: E0 has a signal-quality edge (+0.14 Sharpe at equal sizing; CI includes 0)
SM/LATCH produces higher CAGR at 5% MDD budget (+1.2 pp; CI includes 0)
SM/LATCH produces higher CAGR at 10% MDD budget (+2.3 pp; CI includes 0)
Two-regime model holds in 64-67% of rolling windows
E0 favored in bull markets; SM/LATCH in bear/sideways
Start-date dependence: 2018 bear market matters for LATCH/SM advantage
claims_unsupported: Any statistically proven pairwise Sharpe superiority (all Holm p=1.000)
LATCH complexity premium over SM (Sharpe +0.003, coin flip)
Crossover at ~20% MDD is a stable boundary (varies 3-27%)
do_not_overstate: Do not claim E0 is clearly superior to LATCH/SM based on native score
Do not claim LATCH dominates E0 at low risk as established fact
Do not cite ~20% MDD crossover as a stable law
Do not treat any pairwise Sharpe difference as statistically significant
Do not recommend LATCH over SM without evidence of complexity premium
future_eval_policy: Always separate signal from sizing via factorial decomposition
Always audit exposure and fee drag before comparing strategies
Always validate engine equivalence before comparing implementations
Always use matched-risk frontier for deployment decisions
Never accept complexity increase without robust premium evidence
Always label claims by evidence strength (ROBUST/TENTATIVE/UNSUPPORTED)
Retire current scoring formula from cross-exposure decision authority
